label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Richard could have gone to the bakery. So, first, the problem gives us a timeline of where Richard was at different times, and we need to find the possible time window when he wasn't doing any of those activities, and also before the bakery closed at 7pm. Let's list out the events in order: 1. Richard woke up at 8am. So he's probably starting his day then. 2. Betty saw Richard at the library from 9am to 2pm. So from 9 AM to 2 PM, he's reading there. 3. Then David saw him fixing a computer at the electronic store from 2pm to 4pm. So after 2 PM, he's there until 4 PM. 4. Mary saw him taking photos near the Eiffel Tower from 4pm to 5pm. So right after the electronic store, he's at the Eiffel Tower. 5. Hannah saw him on a rooftop from 5pm to 6pm. So that's next. 6. Susan saw him driving to the water park from 6pm to 7pm. So he's on the road during that hour. 7. The bakery closed after 7pm, so he had to go before that.
